game_name: Home Alone 2: Lost in New York

danger_signals.meaning: Direct threat to Kevin; avoidance or combat required

danger_signals.visual_form: Life counter decrease (standard platformer visual convention)
danger_signals.meaning: Kevin taking damage; approaching game over state

danger_signals.visual_form: Level design obstacles (inferred from platformer genre)

safety_signals.visual_form: Pizza Slices, Whole Pies, Cookies, Bells, Candy Canes, After Shave items
safety_signals.meaning: Health restoration or temporary invulnerability/ability boost

safety_signals.visual_form: Interior locations (Plaza Hotel, Uncle Rob's townhouse)
safety_signals.meaning: Enclosed spaces with reduced immediate threat compared to open streets

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Young boy protagonist sprite (from Home Alone film iconography)
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Plaza Hotel, Central Park, Uncle Rob McCallister's townhouse, New York City streets

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Walking enemies (inferred as burglars from film source material)
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Enemies affected differently by Necklace weapon (slip off-screen mechanic)
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Urban New York City environments where theft occurs

world_state_to_visual.world_state: Kevin in open danger (New York streets)
world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Urban platformer level design with visible enemy sprites
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Enemies actively pursue or patrol; Kevin must fight with Slide, Flying Fist, Super Flying Fist moves or use Dart gun

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Enclosed room/building environments (Plaza Hotel, townhouse interiors)
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Reduced enemy encounter frequency; puzzle or obstacle-focused gameplay

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Health meter visual indication (inferred standard mechanic)
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Kevin moves with reduced capability or visual feedback; powerup collection becomes critical

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Unused elaborate Game Over screen (incomplete in SNES; simple 'Game Over' text overlay used instead)
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: All player input disabled; requires game reset or main menu return

environmental_storytelling.example: Progression through Plaza Hotel, Central Park, Uncle Rob's townhouse, and New York streets mirrors Kevin's journey through Manhattan in the 1992 film

environmental_storytelling.technique: Weapon/tool thematic connection to film scenes
environmental_storytelling.example: Necklace weapon causing enemies to slip off-screen directly references the pearl necklace trap scene from Home Alone 2 film

environmental_storytelling.technique: Powerup visual design for environmental context
environmental_storytelling.example: Food items (Pizza Slices, Whole Pies, Cookies, Candy Canes, After Shave) as survival/health restoration reflect domestic/retail setting of New York City

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Can execute Slide move, Flying Fist, Super Flying Fist, use Dart gun; standard platformer mobility
Requires immediate powerup collection to avoid game over; visual feedback on health meter
Patrol or pursue Kevin; can be affected by Necklace weapon to remove them from level
Slip off-screen; temporary removal from threat landscape

notes: Home Alone 2 SNES uses straightforward platformer visual communication: enemy sprites indicate danger, powerups signal safety/advantage, location changes reflect narrative progression. The game lacks complex faction systems or political elements—conflict is binary (Kevin vs. antagonists). Visual design depends heavily on film recognition rather than in-game world-state indicators. The unused elaborate Game Over screen (complete in Game Boy version) suggests the SNES version was simplified or rushed. World-state communication relies primarily on health meter, enemy presence, and location-based environmental design.
